solution:
def uncommonFromSentences(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> List[str]:
    words1 = s1.split()
    words2 = s2.split()
    uncommon_words = []
    for word1 in words1:
        if word1 not in words2:
            uncommon_words.append(word1)
    for word2 in words2:
        if word2 not in words1:
            uncommon_words.append(word2)
    return uncommon_words

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
This solution uses two nested loops to iterate through the words in both sentences. It checks if each word is present in the other sentence and appends it to the uncommon_words list if it is not found. The time complexity is O(n) because both loops have the same number of iterations, and the space complexity is O(n) due to the list created to store uncommon words. The energy implications are low because the solution only performs a few comparisons and creates a small list.
